opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My chest is incredibly tight, I can't catch a full breath, and my heart is skipping beats. I was reading about connective tissue diseases and I'm convinced my lungs and heart are failing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on tonight after I'd been reading online about a blood test result of mine, and it feels really frightening — I'm scared something is seriously wrong with my heart or lungs.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on suddenly this evening, during and after a long stretch of reading medical articles online about one of my blood results
symptom_narrative.duration: the last hour or so, still going
symptom_narrative.character: a tight, crushing pressure across the chest, like I can't fill my lungs, with my heart racing and skipping
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it feels severe and terrifying — I honestly feel like something awful is about to happen to me
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: across the middle of my chest and my breathing
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started tonight, worse when I focus on it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — I've been trying to breathe slowly
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: reading more about it online and thinking about my organs failing makes it much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: racing heart, feeling like I can't get a full breath, breathing fast, and a strong sense of dread; no jaw or arm pain, no fainting, not coughing up any blood
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had milder tightness and a racing heart before when I've been very anxious about my health, but never this intense
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't settle or think about anything else right now

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a blood test came back with a low-level 'ANA' result that a doctor said was mild and probably nothing serious
past_medical_history.how_managed: no treatment — just told it was a mild finding and to not worry

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: my fingers sometimes go pale and cold in the cold, then come good again
past_medical_history.how_managed: nothing specific — keep my hands warm

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: I get tired more than I used to, but it's mild

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I spend a lot of time reading about health conditions online and I worry a lot about my health

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants definitive proof that her heart and lungs are not failing; strong urge to be told nothing is wrong after reading alarming material online

communication_style.typical_message_style: rapid, fearful messages, quoting things she has read online and asking for reassurance

objective_data_offered.value: around 108 and it feels like it's skipping
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: read off a consumer smartwatch during a highly anxious, hyperventilating episode — rate and 'skipping' sensation are not a diagnostic rhythm assessment

objective_data_offered.value: I'm breathing really fast, maybe 24 breaths a minute
